intro: Nasturtium microphyllum, also known as small-leaved watercress or lesser cress, is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. The plant is rich in nutrients and has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of Nasturtium microphyllum include glucosinolates, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into salads and other culinary dishes for its peppery flavor and nutritional value.
